mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market cooling and impending earnings deceleration, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based demand recovery, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening, rising industrial production indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, supporting short-term equity momentum and positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods correlate with negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM growth surpasses 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth that translates to positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which historically precedes broader economic expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict positive 60-day equity returns with a statistically significant Rank IC greater than 0.03 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, decelerating real GDP growth signals peak economic activity and impending recession risks, prompting portfolio de-risking that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth rates during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances.
